subject: Tomahawk chain reading
modern_doctrine: Tomahawk = blurry ducking torque / stepping ducking paradox torque (modern Red pt11)
resolution_recommendation: surface to Red Wave 2: paradoxMoves reads Tomahawk as 5-ADD compound ducking(+1) + paradox(+1) + whirl(3) = 5; modern reads it as a paradox-torque structural compound — different structures yielding same ADD
notes: Both readings produce 5 ADD but via different decomposition. Modern doctrine subsumes Tomahawk into the paradox-torque family; source treats it as a paradox-whirl compound. Material doctrinal divergence

subject: Pogo-class ADD math
modern_doctrine: pogo(+0) + paradox(+1) + mirage(2) = 3 ADD by modern multiplier doctrine
resolution_recommendation: surface to Red Wave 2: pogo-class compounds in paradoxMoves have ADD values that exceed the multiplier-doctrine derivation by +1 across multiple examples. Pogo here may mean 'pogo+symposium' (+1) rather than pogo-only (+0)
notes: This affects 5 source compounds: Pogo Paradox Mirage Pogo Paradox Barrage Pogo Paradox Drifter Pogo Paradox Eggbeater Pogo Paradox Whirl. Each is 1 ADD higher than modern pogo(+0) doctrine would predict. Systematic pattern suggests source uses 'pogo' loosely for 'pogo+symposium' or 'no-plant element +1'

subject: Paradox Blur vs Blur naming

modern_doctrine: Modern has only blur (chain reading 'stepping paradox mirage')
resolution_recommendation: surface to Red Wave 2: source has TWO entries (Paradox Blur and Blur (Stepping Paradox Mirage)) with subtly different operational notations. Are these the same trick (modern) or distinct (source)?
notes: Paradox Blur operational: SAME IN [PDX] [DEX] > OP IN [DEX]. Blur operational: OP IN [DEX] > OP IN [PDX] [DEX]. Same tokens different sequence. Possibly: source treats paradox-from-setting-leg vs paradox-from-stepping-leg as distinct
